title: The Moon: reading poetry; The Beautiful, Mysterious Moon
description: 2nd grade reading comprehension practice using poetry
topic_path: The Moon: reading poetry; The Beautiful, Mysterious Moon

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question. 

___

##The Beautiful Mysterious Moon##

![Carlos reading a book and the poem's speaker smiling while the moon shines in the background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3bff695453cf0a28e91008ba6f95726f.png)
 
I thought there was a rabbit inside the moon,     
One that nibbled on lettuce and __mushrooms__.    
But Carlos says that those are just craters.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was a giant in the sea    
That made the waves and pushed them onto the beach.    
But Carlos says that those are just tides.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was an old lady under the underground   
With a giant magnet that held us in place when we walk around.   
But Carlos says that it’s just gravity.   

*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*

I don’t know if I agree or disagree,   
That the moon is responsible for tides and gravity.   
But Carlos read the science book, so we’ll have to believe   
That thing in the sky has an effect on you and me.   

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice question##

**What TWO reasons explain why the poet uses the word __mushrooms__ in the second line of the poem?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because the *oo* sound in __mushroom__ is the same sound as the *oo* in “moon”
because the top of a mushroom is round, like a moon crater
because the moon tastes like __mushrooms__
because the *ush* sound in __mushroom__ rhymes  with the *uce* sound in  “lettuce”
because there are __mushrooms__ growing on the moon

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question. 

___

##The Beautiful Mysterious Moon##

![Carlos reading a book and the poem's speaker smiling while the moon shines in the background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3bff695453cf0a28e91008ba6f95726f.png)
 
I thought there was a rabbit inside the moon,     
One that nibbled on lettuce and mushrooms.    
But Carlos says that those are just craters.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was a giant in the sea   
That made the waves and pushed them onto the beach.    
But Carlos says that those are just tides.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was an old lady under the underground   
With a giant magnet that held us in place when we walk __around__.   
But Carlos says that it’s just gravity.   

*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*

I don’t know if I agree or disagree,   
That the moon is responsible for tides and gravity.   
But Carlos read the science book, so we’ll have to believe   
That thing in the sky has an effect on you and me.   

[[☃ explanation 2]]

___

##Practice question##

Read these lines from the poem.

“I thought there was an old lady under the underground   
With a giant magnet that held us in place when we walk __around__.”

**What TWO reasons explain why the poet uses the word __around__?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because it rhymes with “underground”
to help show readers what gravity does
because it rhymes with “magnet”
to help show readers what craters are
because it rhymes with “thought”

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question. 

___

___

##Practice question##

Read the line from the poem. 

“*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*”

**What TWO reasons explain why the poet repeats this line?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to repeat the same sounds, like a song
to highlight that the moon can seem magical
to compare the moon to a ball
to repeat a story about the moon
to explain why the moon has craters

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**What TWO things do we learn at the beginning of the poem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: that the speaker of the poem has ideas about the moon
that the poem is about the moon
that the moon causes gravity
that Carlos likes to read science books
that the moon has food on it

that the speaker is not sure what to believe
that Carlos understands what craters are

##Practice question##

**What does the beginning of the poem tell us about the rest of the poem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It shows us that the speaker and Carlos have different ideas about the moon .
that Carlos reads science books
that the speaker and Carlos both like to go to the beach
that the speaker wants to go to the moon

##Practice question##

Read these lines from the poem. 

“I don’t know if I agree or disagree,  
That the moon is responsible for tides and gravity.”

Read the sentence, then select the correct answer from the dropdown menu.

**At this part of the poem, the speaker feels [[☃ dropdown 1]].**

choices.content: brave
sleepy
unsure
afraid

**What does Carlos do when he hears the speaker’s ideas about the moon?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

He tells the speaker that the moon is beautiful and mysterious.
He tells the speaker that the moon has mushrooms on it.

##Practice question##

**What is the message of this poem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nature can seem magical and is interesting to learn about.
Spending time outside is good for you.
It is important to have dreams.
Reading books can be hard, but you can learn a lot.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**What is the central idea of the poem?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a lot to learn about the moon, but it can still seem magical.
The moon has circles on its surface that look like mushrooms, but they are really craters.
The moon makes the tides at the beach rise and fall.
There is a lot to learn about the moon because most of the things we read in science books are untrue.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**Why is Carlos important to the poem?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He helps us understand that there is a lot to learn about the moon.
He tells us things that help us understand that the moon is very far away.
He wants us to feel happy about living on Earth.
He makes up fun stories about the moon for us to enjoy.
